title: AFI EvidenceRef v1 (District 2 M1 DRAFT)
description: DRAFT / NON-IMPLEMENTATION reference to a piece of evidence backing a signal, enrichment lane, or provenance record. Hash-only by construction: an EvidenceRef carries identifiers, a CanonicalHash of the evidence payload, and normalized evidence/domain timestamps — it MUST NOT and structurally CANNOT require raw payload disclosure (additionalProperties:false, no payload field).
$comment: Timestamps here (fetchedAt/asOf/postedAt/observedAt) are normalized evidence/domain timestamps per District 2 hash doctrine — they describe the evidence itself, not runtime processing, and are therefore admissible in canonical objects. Storage/runtime timestamps (createdAt, storedAt, processedAt, ...) are rejected by additionalProperties:false.
x-afiPartOf: AFI District 2 — Canonical Data & Provenance Boundary (D2 M1)
x-afiDoctrineRefs: District Two M1 provenance-schema authorization — R1-GOV D-R1-5 (afi-config schema drafts and tests only; no runtime wiring)

x-afiOpenItems.ER-O1: Evidence id namespace/format — OPEN; opaque non-empty string in this draft.

evidenceId.description: Stable unique identifier for this piece of evidence.

sourceRef.description: Reference to the source the evidence came from (a sourceId as described by a SourceDisclosureProfile v1).

evidenceHash.description: CanonicalHash v1 of the evidence payload. The payload itself stays off-object; only the digest is referenced.

fetchedAt.description: Normalized evidence timestamp: when the evidence was fetched from the source (ISO 8601), as supplied by the fetch record, not runtime wall-clock.

asOf.description: Normalized domain timestamp: the point in time the evidence content is valid for (e.g. candle close, statement date).

postedAt.description: Normalized evidence timestamp: when the evidence was posted/published at its origin (e.g. message postedAt).

observedAt.description: Normalized evidence timestamp: observation time of the underlying measurement, where distinct from fetch/post time.

uri.description: OPTIONAL pointer to the evidence payload (SHOULD be content-addressed so it is tamper-evident against evidenceHash). Never required — disclosure is governed by the source's SourceDisclosureProfile.

mediaType.description: OPTIONAL media/content type of the evidence payload (e.g. 'application/json', 'text/plain').

redactionStatus.description: OPTIONAL redaction/withholding indicator for the evidence payload. 'disclosed' = payload retrievable; 'redacted' = partially removed; 'withheld' = payload not disclosed (hash-only).

withheldReason.description: OPTIONAL human-readable reason when redactionStatus is 'redacted' or 'withheld'.

notes.description: OPTIONAL free-form provenance notes about this evidence reference.
